channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: eThekwini resolves long-standing contract issues to improve traffic fine processing
item.description: The eThekwini Municipality has finally resolved a seven-year legal dispute with a service provider that significantly hampered its ability to process traffic fines. The dispute, which began after a 20...

item.title: Yanga’s Championship Hopes Rely on Key Matches with Azam FC
item.description: Dar es Salaam – Defending champions Young Africans SC, commonly known as Yanga, are entering one of the most critical periods of their season. Their ambitions to retain both the CRDB Federation Cup an...

item.title: Putin and Xi hold talks in Beijing to strengthen strategic partnership amid ongoing geopolitical tensions
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin has arrived in Beijing to meet with Chinese President Xi Jinping, just days after former US President Donald Trump's visit to the Chinese capital. The meeting aims to ...

item.title: Trump and Xi Agree on Managed Rivalry and Strategic Stability Between the US and China
item.description: In a historic meeting in Beijing, US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping signaled a new approach to international relations, moving away from the traditional narrative of rivalry o...

item.title: Delta police neutralise suspected kidnapper during forest operation
item.description: For Delta State, security no dey take sleep as police from the State Anti-Kidnapping Unit (SAKU) join local volunteers to conduct one serious operation against suspected kidnappers. According to Brigh...

item.title: Tanzania starts construction of Sh241 billion six-lane highway to ease traffic in Dodoma
item.description: Dodoma city is expected to see big relief from morning and evening traffic congestion following the launch of a major road project. The government has started the Sh241 billion initiative to upgrade t...

item.title: Mad Carpentier: Post-War French Haute Couture House Known for Elegant Draping and Architectural Designs
item.description: Mad Carpentier was a notable French haute couture house established in 1940 by Mad Maltezos and Suzy Carpentier. Active primarily during the 1940s and 1950s, the label became celebrated for its sophis...

item.title: LSK Calls for Full Audit of Kenya's G-to-G Fuel Procurement Amid Price Disputes
item.description: The Law Society of Kenya (LSK) has called for a full forensic and public audit of the Government-to-Government (G-to-G) fuel importation framework, citing transparency and accountability concerns. Thi...

item.title: Pasig Prosecutor's Office Dismisses Frustrated Murder and Malicious Mischief Cases Against PNP Officer in Viral Road Rage Incident
item.description: The Pasig City Prosecutor's Office has dismissed the complaints for frustrated murder and malicious mischief filed against a police personnel involved in a viral road incident video. According to the ...

item.title: California Doctor Convicted for $45 Million Medicare Fraud Scheme
item.description: Violetta Mailyan, a 45-year-old doctor from Glendale, California, has been convicted of orchestrating a $45 million Medicare fraud scheme. The federal jury found her guilty on nine counts of wire frau...

item.title: Kylie Minogue Clarifies She Will Not Feature on Madonna’s ‘Confessions II’
item.description: Kylie Minogue has addressed speculation regarding a potential collaboration with Madonna on the latter’s forthcoming album, ‘Confessions II’. The rumours began circulating after the two pop icons shar...

item.title: Mamelodi Sundowns delayed from flying to Rabat after Morocco denies landing permit
item.description: Mamelodi Sundowns’ preparations for the second leg of the CAF Champions League final against AS FAR in Rabat have been seriously disrupted. The team was ready to depart South Africa on Tuesday morning...

item.title: Police say outsiders were brought in during Kitengela fuel protests, 111 in custody
item.description: On Monday, what was expected to be a peaceful protest against rising fuel prices in Kitengela town turned chaotic, with reports indicating that groups of outsiders were brought in early in the morning...

item.title: Lawyer accused of killing colleague over whisky dispute appears in court
item.description: An Eldoret court matter involving lawyer Abel Mogaka has drawn attention after he was accused of killing his colleague Calvin Musachi Ngaira during a fight linked to a whisky dispute. The case centres...

item.title: Nigeria and Madagascar key opponents for Taifa Stars in AFCON qualifiers
item.description: Tanzania’s national football team, Taifa Stars, is preparing for the upcoming Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON) qualifiers, where they will face tough opponents including Nigeria and Madagascar. These two...

item.title: NPS Clarifies That Central Police Station OCS Is Not Facing Court Arraignment
item.description: The National Police Service has come out clearly to dismiss social media rumours claiming that the Officer Commanding Station at Nairobi's Central Police Station was about to be arraigned in court. Ch...

item.title: Election watchdogs appeal Comelec ruling on alleged prohibited campaign donations by Marcos and Duterte
item.description: Election watchdog group Kontra Daya, together with other sectoral organizations, has filed an appeal against the Commission on Elections (Comelec) ruling regarding alleged prohibited campaign contribu...

item.title: Portraits Highlighting Edwardian Women’s Fashion and Social Grace
item.description: The Edwardian Era, spanning the early 1900s, was a period marked by elegance, sophistication, and a new sense of feminine grace. This era is often remembered for the iconic “S-bend” silhouette and the...

item.title: Rising Costs of Essentials Put Pressure on South African Households Despite Economic Stability
item.description: South Africa has managed to maintain macroeconomic stability, but ordinary households are feeling the pinch as prices of essential items such as food, transport, and electricity continue to rise. The ...

item.title: Rio Ferdinand warmly received during Tanzania visit
item.description: Former England football captain Rio Ferdinand recently visited Tanzania, where he received a very warm and heroic welcome from fans and locals alike. His visit coincided with several sporting and cult...
